description:
Recommendation anchor: OBS-01b in docs/refactor/src-agent-platform-transfer-recommendations.md.

Add lightweight structured telemetry for turn duration, outcome reason, loop blocks, and fallback metadata so runtime state is inspectable without introducing a heavy analytics stack.

Why this bead exists:
- src makes turn outcome/status explicit in its final result handling (`src/QueryEngine.ts:1040-1129`).
- metiq already persists session metadata and exposes an event bus, so it has good seams for operational telemetry (`swarmstr/internal/store/state/session_store.go`, `swarmstr/internal/gateway/ws/event_bus.go`).
- This bead complements OBS-01a and STATE-01a by making outcome state observable and durable.

Scope:
- Record a minimal set of structured turn metrics and outcome fields.
- Keep the telemetry local to operational/runtime debugging and introspection.
- Align naming with the turn outcome taxonomy.

Non-goals:
- Do not add product analytics, dashboards, or vendor-specific tracing.
- Do not duplicate metrics that are already persisted unless the duplication is justified.

- This bead transfers the useful lifecycle/inheritance idea from src while preserving ACP as the multi-agent boundary.

Scope:
- Add structured inheritance fields to ACP task payloads.
- Allow workers to reconstruct intended runtime constraints from the task envelope.
- Keep the protocol Go-native and ACP-native.

Non-goals:
- Do not recreate src's same-process subagent model.
- Do not overhaul ACP transport semantics.

description:
Recommendation anchor: OBS-01a in docs/refactor/src-agent-platform-transfer-recommendations.md.

Add explicit tool lifecycle events to the event bus so tool execution can be observed through structured runtime events rather than logs alone.

Why this bead exists:
- src emits progress/result updates throughout tool execution (`src/services/tools/toolExecution.ts:337-576`).
- metiq already has a strong event bus boundary, but the selected event taxonomy focuses on agent/chat/plugin events and does not clearly include dedicated tool lifecycle events (`swarmstr/internal/gateway/ws/event_bus.go`).
- This bead turns later scheduler/policy work into inspectable runtime behavior.

Scope:
- Add tool start/progress/result/error style event types.
- Ensure events carry enough metadata to correlate them with turns and tool calls.
- Keep telemetry operational and server-side.

description:
Recommendation anchor: LOOP-01a in docs/refactor/src-agent-platform-transfer-recommendations.md.

Replace metiq's unconditional parallel tool execution with partitioned scheduling: run declared-safe query/read tools concurrently and serialize mutating/destructive tools.

Why this bead exists:
- src partitions tool calls into concurrency-safe batches and serial batches (`src/services/tools/toolOrchestration.ts:19-188`).
- metiq's shared loop currently executes all tool calls in parallel (`swarmstr/internal/agent/agentic_loop.go:126-284`).
- Partitioned scheduling is one of the highest-value portable behaviors from src because it reduces races without abandoning concurrency completely.

Scope:
- Introduce a scheduler that uses descriptor traits to group or serialize calls.
- Preserve result ordering.
- Keep the implementation local to the shared loop rather than distributing scheduling logic across providers.

Non-goals:
- Do not change ACP topology.
- Do not add streaming-time execution.
